title: Spanish B1-B2 Activation Quiz
subtitle: 50 practical Spanish vocabulary activation questions
description: A 50-question mixed quiz for turning passive Spanish vocabulary into active usage through realistic daily, professional, academic, and social contexts.

questions.situation: You bought headphones last week and they already stopped working.
questions.sentence: Compré estos auriculares hace una semana y ya han dejado de {0}.

feedback.natural: Dejar de funcionar is the standard phrase for devices that stop working.
feedback.alternatives: Trabajar is an English transfer here. Servir can mean to be useful, but not the clean product-failure phrase.
feedback.chunk: Ha dejado de funcionar is essential for complaints and customer service.

takeaway.title: Device complaint phrase
takeaway.body: "Dejar de funcionar" is the natural phrase for a product or device that stops working.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: Compré estos auriculares hace una semana y ya han dejado de funcionar.

questions.audio_text: Compré estos auriculares hace una semana y ya han dejado de funcionar.

questions.situation: At a hotel, your assigned room is not the one you booked.
questions.sentence: Creo que hay un {0} con mi reserva. Pedí una habitación con {1} al mar.

feedback.natural: Hay un error con mi reserva is polite and clear. Con vistas al mar is the fixed hotel phrase.
feedback.alternatives: Conflicto sounds too strong. Mirada means a look, not a view from a room.
feedback.chunk: Creo que hay un error con mi reserva is a useful complaint opener.

takeaway.title: Hotel problem opener
takeaway.body: "Hay un error con mi reserva" and "con vistas al mar" are practical hotel phrases.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: Creo que hay un error con mi reserva. Pedí una habitación con vistas al mar.

questions.audio_text: Creo que hay un error con mi reserva. Pedí una habitación con vistas al mar.

questions.situation: You want to soften your opinion in a meeting.
questions.prompt: ___ la propuesta necesita más datos antes de aprobarse.

choices.text: Me da la impresión de que

feedback.natural: Me da la impresión de que softens an opinion and sounds diplomatic.
feedback.alternatives: Me cae bien is for liking people, me hace falta expresses need, and me toca means it is my turn.
feedback.chunk: Me da la impresión de que... is useful when you want to disagree carefully.

takeaway.title: Soft opinion phrase
takeaway.body: "Me da la impresión de que..." makes an opinion sound less blunt.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: Me da la impresión de que la propuesta necesita más datos antes de aprobarse.

questions.audio_text: Me da la impresión de que la propuesta necesita más datos antes de aprobarse.

questions.situation: You are telling a classmate that a deadline matters for the project.
questions.sentence: Tenemos que {0} en cuenta la fecha de entrega antes de repartir las tareas.

feedback.natural: Tener en cuenta means to take something into account and is extremely common.
feedback.alternatives: Hacer en cuenta and dar en cuenta are not natural. Tomar en cuenta also works in many varieties.
feedback.chunk: Tener en cuenta + noun is useful in academic and work planning.

takeaway.title: Take into account
takeaway.body: "Tener en cuenta" is the go-to phrase for considering an important factor.

questions.situation: You and a friend need to decide a time that works for both of you.
questions.sentence: Tenemos que {0} de acuerdo sobre la hora antes de reservar la mesa.

feedback.natural: Ponernos de acuerdo means to agree or reach an agreement together.
feedback.alternatives: Hacernos de acuerdo is not natural. Estar de acuerdo describes agreement, but here the process of agreeing is better.
feedback.chunk: Ponerse de acuerdo is perfect for scheduling and group decisions.

takeaway.title: Reach agreement
takeaway.body: "Ponerse de acuerdo" means to agree on something after coordinating.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: Tenemos que ponernos de acuerdo sobre la hora antes de reservar la mesa.

questions.audio_text: Tenemos que ponernos de acuerdo sobre la hora antes de reservar la mesa.

questions.situation: Your team is talking about executing a plan.
questions.prompt: El proyecto es viable, pero necesitamos un equipo capaz de ___ el plan.

feedback.natural: Llevar a cabo means to carry out or execute a plan.
feedback.alternatives: Llevar encima means to carry on you, llevarse bien means to get along, and llevar por delante has a different meaning.
feedback.chunk: Llevar a cabo un plan/proyecto/iniciativa is a strong professional collocation.

takeaway.title: Execute a plan
takeaway.body: "Llevar a cabo" is the natural verb phrase for carrying out a project or plan.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: El proyecto es viable, pero necesitamos un equipo capaz de llevar a cabo el plan.

questions.audio_text: El proyecto es viable, pero necesitamos un equipo capaz de llevar a cabo el plan.

questions.situation: You want to say a museum is worth visiting.
questions.sentence: Aunque está un poco lejos, {0} la pena visitar ese museo.

feedback.natural: Merece la pena means it is worth it and sounds natural in recommendations.
feedback.alternatives: Tiene la pena is wrong. Vale la pena also works, especially in many varieties.
feedback.chunk: Merece la pena + infinitive is high-frequency for advice.

takeaway.title: Worth doing
takeaway.body: "Merece la pena visitar..." is a practical recommendation phrase.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: Aunque está un poco lejos, merece la pena visitar ese museo.

questions.audio_text: Aunque está un poco lejos, merece la pena visitar ese museo.

questions.situation: You are explaining how one event caused changes in a company.
questions.sentence: {0} raíz de la crisis, la empresa decidió reducir gastos.

feedback.natural: A raíz de means as a result of or following an event.
feedback.alternatives: En raíz de and con raíz de are not fixed expressions. Por alone would require a different structure.
feedback.chunk: A raíz de + noun is useful in news, reports, and explanations.

takeaway.title: As a result of
takeaway.body: "A raíz de" introduces the event that triggered a later consequence.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: a raíz de la crisis, la empresa decidió reducir gastos.

questions.audio_text: a raíz de la crisis, la empresa decidió reducir gastos.

questions.situation: You are proposing a meeting time politely.
questions.sentence: ¿Te {0} bien quedar mañana por la tarde para revisar el trabajo?

feedback.natural: Te vendría bien is a natural way to ask if a time works for someone.
feedback.alternatives: Caería, saldría, and pasaría do not fit this scheduling phrase.
feedback.chunk: ¿Te vendría bien...? is polite and useful for meetings and plans.

takeaway.title: Polite scheduling
takeaway.body: "¿Te vendría bien...?" is a natural way to ask if something would suit someone.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: ¿Te vendría bien quedar mañana por la tarde para revisar el trabajo?

questions.audio_text: ¿Te vendría bien quedar mañana por la tarde para revisar el trabajo?

questions.situation: You want to say you ran out of coffee at home.
questions.sentence: Esta mañana me he quedado {0} café, así que he tenido que salir a comprar.

feedback.natural: Quedarse sin means to run out of something.
feedback.alternatives: The other prepositions do not form the expression for running out.
feedback.chunk: Me he quedado sin... is useful for money, battery, time, food, and supplies.

takeaway.title: Run out of something
takeaway.body: "Quedarse sin" means to run out of something you need.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: Esta mañana me he quedado sin café, así que he tenido que salir a comprar.

questions.audio_text: Esta mañana me he quedado sin café, así que he tenido que salir a comprar.

questions.situation: During class, you want to say you have a question in a formal way.
questions.prompt: Perdón, me gustaría ___ una duda sobre la lectura.

feedback.natural: Plantear una duda is a formal and natural academic collocation.
feedback.alternatives: Levantar una duda is not the normal collocation. The other verbs do not fit the meaning.
feedback.chunk: Plantear una duda/un problema/una cuestión is useful in class and meetings.

takeaway.title: Raise a question
takeaway.body: "Plantear una duda" is a polished way to raise a question.

questions.situation: You are presenting and want to emphasize one important point.
questions.sentence: Quisiera hacer {0} en la importancia de revisar los datos antes de publicarlos.

feedback.natural: Hacer hincapié en means to emphasize and is common in presentations.
feedback.alternatives: Hacer énfasis en can work, but hacer hincapié en is the stronger fixed phrase here.
feedback.chunk: Hacer hincapié en + noun is useful in academic and professional speaking.

takeaway.title: Emphasize a point
takeaway.body: "Hacer hincapié en" means to emphasize something important.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: Quisiera hacer hincapié en la importancia de revisar los datos antes de publicarlos.

questions.audio_text: Quisiera hacer hincapié en la importancia de revisar los datos antes de publicarlos.

questions.situation: You want to justify a small mistake by saying everyone is learning.
questions.sentence: No pasa nada; {0} fin y al cabo, todos estamos aprendiendo.

feedback.natural: Al fin y al cabo means after all and introduces a final justification.
feedback.alternatives: The phrase is fixed with al; the other prepositions break it.
feedback.chunk: Al fin y al cabo... is common when giving perspective or softening a point.

takeaway.title: After all
takeaway.body: "Al fin y al cabo" means after all and helps you justify or reframe something.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: No pasa nada; al fin y al cabo, todos estamos aprendiendo.

questions.audio_text: No pasa nada; al fin y al cabo, todos estamos aprendiendo.

questions.situation: You want to say you agree with a coworker.
questions.prompt: Estoy de acuerdo ___ lo que ha dicho Marta.

feedback.natural: Estar de acuerdo con is the correct verb-preposition pattern.
feedback.alternatives: The other prepositions are common in other structures, but not after estar de acuerdo.
feedback.chunk: Estoy de acuerdo con... is a high-frequency discussion phrase.

takeaway.title: Agree with
takeaway.body: "Estar de acuerdo con" is the standard pattern for agreeing with someone or something.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: Estoy de acuerdo con lo que ha dicho Marta.

questions.audio_text: Estoy de acuerdo con lo que ha dicho Marta.

questions.situation: You did not understand one part of an explanation.
questions.sentence: Perdona, no me {0} claro qué tenemos que entregar exactamente.

feedback.natural: No me queda claro means it is not clear to me and sounds natural.
feedback.alternatives: No me deja claro would need a subject that explains something. No me sale claro is not natural.
feedback.chunk: No me queda claro... is useful when asking for clarification politely.

takeaway.title: Ask for clarification
takeaway.body: "No me queda claro..." is a natural way to say something is not clear to you.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: Perdona, no me queda claro qué tenemos que entregar exactamente.

questions.audio_text: Perdona, no me queda claro qué tenemos que entregar exactamente.

questions.situation: A manager says the company must act after repeated complaints.
questions.sentence: Si seguimos recibiendo quejas, tendremos que tomar {0}.

feedback.natural: Tomar medidas means to take measures and is common in formal problem-solving.
feedback.alternatives: Tomar acciones is understandable but less idiomatic in Spain. Pasos can work in other contexts but not as fixed here.
feedback.chunk: Tomar medidas is useful for problems, risks, and complaints.

takeaway.title: Take measures
takeaway.body: "Tomar medidas" is the natural collocation for acting in response to a problem.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: Si seguimos recibiendo quejas, tendremos que tomar medidas.

questions.audio_text: Si seguimos recibiendo quejas, tendremos que tomar medidas.

questions.situation: You are changing topic in a meeting agenda.
questions.prompt: ___ la nueva campaña, todavía no hemos recibido el presupuesto final.

feedback.natural: En cuanto a introduces or shifts to a topic: as for / regarding.
feedback.alternatives: Debido a gives a cause, a pesar de gives contrast, and con tal de gives purpose/condition.
feedback.chunk: En cuanto a... is useful for emails, reports, and meetings.

takeaway.title: Regarding a topic
takeaway.body: "En cuanto a" means regarding or as for, and helps organize your speech.

es-ES-AlvaroNeural.audio_text: En cuanto a la nueva campaña, todavía no hemos recibido el presupuesto final.

questions.audio_text: En cuanto a la nueva campaña, todavía no hemos recibido el presupuesto final.

questions.situation: You assumed your roommate had paid the bill, but they had not.
questions.sentence: Di por {0} que ya habías pagado la factura.

feedback.natural: Dar por hecho means to take for granted or assume.
feedback.alternatives: The other participles do not form this expression.
feedback.chunk: Di por hecho que... is useful when explaining a mistaken assumption.

takeaway.title: Assume
takeaway.body: "Dar por hecho que..." means to assume something without checking.

questions.situation: You need a document soon and want to sound professional.
questions.sentence: ¿Podrías enviarme el documento {0} antes posible?

feedback.natural: Lo antes posible means as soon as possible.
feedback.alternatives: The phrase is fixed with lo; the other articles do not work.
feedback.chunk: Lo antes posible is a practical phrase for requests and deadlines.

takeaway.title: As soon as possible
takeaway.body: "Lo antes posible" is the standard phrase for ASAP.

questions.situation: You want to bring an umbrella because it might rain.
questions.prompt: Voy a llevar paraguas ___ llueve por la tarde.

feedback.natural: Por si acaso means just in case and expresses precaution.
feedback.alternatives: Por lo tanto gives a result, a cambio de means in exchange for, and en lugar de means instead of.
feedback.chunk: Por si acaso is very common in everyday planning.

takeaway.title: Just in case
takeaway.body: "Por si acaso" is the natural phrase for precautions.

questions.situation: You are discussing the future impact of a decision.
questions.sentence: A {0} plazo, esta inversión puede ahorrar mucho dinero.

feedback.natural: A largo plazo means in the long term.
feedback.alternatives: The fixed expression uses largo, not grande or alto.
feedback.chunk: A corto/medio/largo plazo is useful for planning and analysis.

takeaway.title: Long term
takeaway.body: "A largo plazo" means in the long term.

questions.situation: A friend tells you they found a job, and you respond warmly.
questions.sentence: Me alegra que hayas {0} trabajo tan rápido.

feedback.natural: Me alegra que hayas encontrado combines an emotion phrase with the subjunctive perfect.
feedback.alternatives: Buscado means looked for, not found. Pedido means requested.
feedback.chunk: Me alegra que hayas... is useful for reacting to good news.

takeaway.title: Reacting to good news
takeaway.body: "Me alegra que hayas..." is a natural way to say you are happy someone did something.

questions.situation: You want to correct a negative statement: not this, but that.
questions.prompt: No quiero cancelar la reunión, ___ cambiarla a otra hora.

feedback.natural: Sino corrects a previous negative idea: not cancel, but reschedule.
feedback.alternatives: Pero contrasts, but after a negated correction Spanish normally needs sino.
feedback.chunk: No X, sino Y is essential for precise correction.

takeaway.title: Not X, but Y
takeaway.body: "No..., sino..." is the structure for correcting a negative idea.

questions.situation: Your team is launching a new online service.
questions.sentence: La empresa va a poner en {0} una nueva plataforma de reservas.

feedback.natural: Poner en marcha means to launch or set something in motion.
feedback.alternatives: The other nouns do not form the business/project collocation.
feedback.chunk: Poner en marcha un proyecto/servicio/plan is very useful professionally.

takeaway.title: Launch a project
takeaway.body: "Poner en marcha" means to start or launch a project, plan, or service.

questions.situation: You cannot stop thinking about a decision.
questions.sentence: Llevo toda la noche dándole {0} al tema de cambiar de trabajo.

feedback.natural: Darle vueltas a algo means to keep thinking about something.
feedback.alternatives: The phrase is fixed with vueltas; the literal alternatives sound unnatural.
feedback.chunk: Darle vueltas a un tema is common for worries and decisions.

takeaway.title: Keep thinking about it
takeaway.body: "Darle vueltas a algo" means to keep thinking about an issue.

questions.situation: A budget is not enough for what the project needs.
questions.prompt: Con ese presupuesto nos vamos a ___ para cubrir todos los gastos.

feedback.natural: Quedarse corto means to fall short or not be enough.
feedback.alternatives: The other adjective combinations do not express insufficient quantity.
feedback.chunk: Nos quedamos cortos is useful for budgets, time, estimates, and explanations.

takeaway.title: Fall short
takeaway.body: "Quedarse corto" means to be insufficient or underestimate something.

questions.situation: You are introducing a topic in a formal email.
questions.sentence: {0} respecto a su solicitud, le enviaremos una respuesta esta semana.

feedback.natural: Con respecto a means regarding and is useful in formal writing.
feedback.alternatives: The phrase is fixed with con. Respecto a can also work without con, but not with the other prepositions.
feedback.chunk: Con respecto a... organizes formal emails and reports.

takeaway.title: Regarding
takeaway.body: "Con respecto a" is a formal way to introduce the topic you are addressing.

questions.situation: It was raining, but you went out anyway.
questions.sentence: Estaba lloviendo mucho. Aun {0}, salimos a cenar.

feedback.natural: Aun así means even so or nevertheless.
feedback.alternatives: The phrase is fixed. The other words do not complete this contrast connector.
feedback.chunk: Aun así... is common in spoken and written Spanish.

takeaway.title: Even so
takeaway.body: "Aun así" means even so and introduces an action despite a problem.

questions.situation: A school needs to deal with a rise in student stress.
questions.prompt: La universidad debe ___ el aumento del estrés entre los estudiantes.

feedback.natural: Hacer frente a means to face or deal with a problem.
feedback.alternatives: Hacer caso a means to pay attention to, hacer falta means to be necessary, and hacer daño means to hurt.
feedback.chunk: Hacer frente a un problema/desafío/crisis is very useful.

takeaway.title: Deal with a problem
takeaway.body: "Hacer frente a" means to confront or deal with a problem.

questions.situation: You are waiting for an email and checking your inbox often.
questions.sentence: Estoy pendiente {0} tu respuesta para confirmar la reserva.

feedback.natural: Estar pendiente de means to be waiting for or keeping an eye on something.
feedback.alternatives: The expression takes de, not the other prepositions.
feedback.chunk: Estoy pendiente de tu respuesta is useful in messages and emails.

takeaway.title: Waiting for a response
takeaway.body: "Estar pendiente de" means to be watching for or waiting for something.

questions.situation: You want to say you know the schedule is complicated.
questions.sentence: Soy consciente {0} que el horario no es ideal para todos.

feedback.natural: Ser consciente de que is the correct pattern for being aware that something is true.
feedback.alternatives: The adjective consciente needs de before a noun or clause.
feedback.chunk: Soy consciente de que... is useful for polite, responsible communication.

takeaway.title: Be aware that
takeaway.body: "Ser consciente de que..." means to be aware that something is true.

questions.situation: You forgot to send a file and want to admit it naturally.
questions.prompt: Lo siento, ___ enviar el archivo antes de salir.

feedback.natural: Se me ha olvidado is the natural accidental-forgetting structure.
feedback.alternatives: The other options are ungrammatical or mean something different. Se me ha perdido means I lost it.
feedback.chunk: Se me ha olvidado + infinitive is very common and sounds native.

takeaway.title: I forgot accidentally
takeaway.body: "Se me ha olvidado" is the natural way to say something slipped your mind.

questions.situation: You want to make your position very explicit in a discussion.
questions.sentence: Quiero dejar {0} que no estoy en contra de la idea, sino del plazo.

feedback.natural: Dejar claro que means to make clear that something is your position.
feedback.alternatives: The other adjectives do not form this communication phrase.
feedback.chunk: Quiero dejar claro que... is useful in meetings and disagreements.

takeaway.title: Make clear
takeaway.body: "Dejar claro que..." lets you state your position explicitly.

questions.situation: You think a proposal is illogical.
questions.sentence: No tiene {0} gastar más dinero si todavía no sabemos cuál es el problema.

feedback.natural: No tiene sentido means it does not make sense.
feedback.alternatives: No tiene razón means someone is not right, not that an action is illogical.
feedback.chunk: No tiene sentido + infinitive is useful for evaluating decisions.

takeaway.title: Does not make sense
takeaway.body: "No tiene sentido" is the natural phrase for saying something is illogical.

questions.situation: You are explaining a logical consequence.
questions.prompt: No hemos recibido la confirmación. ___, no podemos reservar los billetes todavía.

feedback.natural: Por lo tanto introduces a logical result.
feedback.alternatives: Aunque and a pesar de express contrast, and mientras means while.
feedback.chunk: Por lo tanto... is useful in reports and explanations.

takeaway.title: Therefore
takeaway.body: "Por lo tanto" introduces a consequence or conclusion.

questions.situation: You moved abroad and miss your friends.
questions.sentence: Desde que vivo aquí, echo mucho de {0} a mis amigos.

feedback.natural: Echar de menos a alguien means to miss someone.
feedback.alternatives: Extrañar can work as a verb in many varieties, but not in the phrase echo de ___.
feedback.chunk: Echo de menos a... is common for people, places, and routines.

takeaway.title: Miss someone
takeaway.body: "Echar de menos a alguien" means to miss someone.

questions.situation: You missed class and need to catch up.
questions.sentence: No pude ir a clase la semana pasada, así que tengo que ponerme al {0}.

feedback.natural: Ponerse al día means to catch up.
feedback.alternatives: The expression is fixed with día.
feedback.chunk: Ponerme al día is useful for study, work, and missed information.

takeaway.title: Catch up
takeaway.body: "Ponerse al día" means to catch up after missing work, class, or information.

questions.situation: You want to say writing formal emails is difficult for you.

feedback.natural: Me cuesta + infinitive means I find it difficult to do something.
feedback.alternatives: Me cuesta de adds an unnecessary preposition. The other verbs express lack or remaining, not difficulty.
feedback.chunk: Me cuesta + infinitive is very useful for talking about learning challenges.

takeaway.title: I find it hard to
takeaway.body: "Me cuesta + infinitive" means something is difficult for me.

questions.situation: You are explaining that the problem is not technical, but organizational.
questions.sentence: No se trata {0} un problema técnico, sino de una falta de coordinación.

feedback.natural: Se trata de means it is about or it is a matter of.
feedback.alternatives: The expression takes de.
feedback.chunk: No se trata de X, sino de Y is excellent for precise reformulation.

takeaway.title: It is not about X
takeaway.body: "No se trata de X, sino de Y" is a useful structure for correcting the focus.

questions.situation: Your team completed a difficult project despite problems.
questions.sentence: Con mucho esfuerzo, logramos sacar {0} el proyecto.

feedback.natural: Sacar adelante un proyecto means to push it through successfully.
feedback.alternatives: The other adverbs do not form this achievement phrase.
feedback.chunk: Sacar adelante is useful for work, study, and personal challenges.

takeaway.title: Push through
takeaway.body: "Sacar adelante" means to successfully complete or support something difficult.

questions.situation: You want to say the plan will work only if there are no delays.
questions.prompt: Podemos terminar el proyecto el viernes, ___ surja ningún imprevisto.

feedback.natural: A menos que means unless and triggers subjunctive here.
feedback.alternatives: The other connectors express result or contrast, not an exception condition.
feedback.chunk: A menos que + subjunctive is useful for conditions and plans.

takeaway.title: Unless
takeaway.body: "A menos que" means unless and often goes with the subjunctive.

questions.situation: You suggest calling instead of writing another long email.
questions.sentence: En {0} de mandar otro correo largo, podríamos llamarla directamente.

feedback.natural: En vez de means instead of and fits a practical alternative.
feedback.alternatives: En lugar de also works, but the prompt asks for the fixed phrase with en ___ de.
feedback.chunk: En vez de + infinitive is useful for suggesting alternatives.

takeaway.title: Instead of
takeaway.body: "En vez de" introduces an alternative action.

questions.situation: Two departments finally agree after a long discussion.
questions.sentence: Después de varias reuniones, las dos partes llegaron a un {0}.

feedback.natural: Llegar a un acuerdo means to reach an agreement.
feedback.alternatives: Contrato is a legal document. Trato can mean deal, but the standard collocation is acuerdo.
feedback.chunk: Llegar a un acuerdo is essential for work, negotiation, and relationships.

takeaway.title: Reach an agreement
takeaway.body: "Llegar a un acuerdo" is the natural phrase for reaching agreement.

questions.situation: Your friend looks tired and you want to comment gently.

feedback.natural: Se nota que means it is noticeable that or you can tell that.
feedback.alternatives: The other reflexive combinations do not introduce an observation.
feedback.chunk: Se nota que... is useful for visible signs and impressions.

takeaway.title: You can tell that
takeaway.body: "Se nota que..." means it is noticeable that something is true.

questions.situation: You accidentally ignored an important detail in an assignment.
questions.sentence: Creo que he pasado por {0} un detalle importante del enunciado.

feedback.natural: Pasar por alto means to overlook something.
feedback.alternatives: The expression is fixed with alto.
feedback.chunk: Pasar por alto un detalle is useful for mistakes and review.

takeaway.title: Overlook something
takeaway.body: "Pasar por alto" means to overlook or miss a detail.

questions.situation: You want to say you feel like trying a new restaurant.
questions.sentence: Tengo ganas {0} probar ese restaurante nuevo del centro.

feedback.natural: Tener ganas de means to feel like doing something.
feedback.alternatives: The expression takes de before an infinitive.
feedback.chunk: Tengo ganas de + infinitive is a very common everyday phrase.

takeaway.title: Feel like doing
takeaway.body: "Tener ganas de" means to feel like doing something.

questions.situation: You and a friend decide where and when to meet.
questions.prompt: ¿___ a las siete delante del cine?

feedback.natural: Quedamos means shall we meet / we meet when arranging plans.
feedback.alternatives: Nos quedamos means we stay. Nos queda means we have left or it suits us in another structure.
feedback.chunk: ¿Quedamos a las siete? is a core social planning phrase.

takeaway.title: Make plans
takeaway.body: "¿Quedamos...?" is the natural way to arrange to meet someone.
